subject: Basement flooded AGAIN after Saturday's storm — where is the regrade?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing once more about 1733 Frederick Ave. Saturday night's storm dumped water down the side yard and I took about four inches in the basement — third time in two years. The sump ran all night and still couldn't keep up. We have been talking about the swale regrade and the 50/50 cost-share since the engineer's visit last fall, and I keep being told it is on the capital list. I am 71 years old and I cannot keep hauling ruined boxes up the stairs. Please tell me a real date for the Frederick Ave drainage work, or at least confirm the cost-share number so I can decide whether to pay for the grading myself.

subject: Basement flooded AGAIN after Saturday's storm — where is the regrade?
body_clean: Ms. Meyer, I'm sorry — four inches after everything you've been through is not acceptable. Here is where things actually stand: the Frederick Ave side-yard swale regrade (project DR-26-114) is funded in the FY27 capital budget that takes effect July 1, and our engineer has it scheduled for the August grading window. The cost-share under the residential drainage program would be 50/50, with your estimated share at roughly $1,850 for the regrade and a new catch basin tie-in. I do not want you paying out of pocket for work the village is about to do. I'll have the engineer call you this week to walk the yard again and confirm the scope. In the meantime, file a claim photo log for Saturday's damage.

subject: Bohland Ave still ponding — storm sewer not keeping up
body_clean: Mayor, following up on the drainage problem at 2218 Bohland Ave. After Saturday's rain the street out front held water curb to curb for almost two hours, and it backed up into my garage apron again. The new restrictor the village put on the storm inlet last year was supposed to fix the surcharging but honestly it feels worse — the water has nowhere to go. My neighbor at 2224 got water in his crawlspace too. Can Public Works come jet the line on our block and check whether that inlet is partially blocked? I've got photos and a video from Saturday timestamped if that helps the work order.

subject: Thank you — and a quick question on my June water bill
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, first I want to say thank you. The young man from the water department, Andre, came out last Thursday and found the leaking toilet flapper that was running up my bill, and he didn't charge me a thing for looking. That is the kind of service that makes me proud to have lived in Bellwood for 38 years. My one question: my June bill (account 25-0418) still shows the higher usage from before the fix. Will the next bill straighten out on its own, or do I need to do something? I don't mind paying for water I used, I just want to make sure the meter is reading right now. Bless you all.

subject: Thank you — and a quick question on my June water bill
body_clean: Mrs. Bennett, what a kind note — I'll make sure Andre hears it directly, that means a lot to the crew. On the bill: you're right that June account 25-0418 still reflects the pre-repair usage because the meter reads ran before the flapper was replaced. You don't need to do anything. Our water clerk will apply a one-time leak adjustment to the affected period and the July bill will reflect your normal usage. If anything looks off on July's statement, call the office and ask for me by name. Thirty-eight years — you're exactly the neighbor this village is for. Thank you, Gloria.

subject: Bar noise until 2am AGAIN this weekend — 511 St. Charles
body_clean: Mayor, I am at the end of my rope. Saturday night the music and the crowd outside The Hideout went until past 2am — I clocked the last car door slamming at 2:18. Bass through my bedroom wall at 511 St. Charles, shouting in the parking lot, and someone broke a bottle on the sidewalk right under my window. This is the fourth weekend in a row I've documented. I called the non-emergency line at 1:05am and was told a car would come by; if it did, nothing changed. I have a noise log with dates and times going back to May. When does the village actually enforce the 11pm outdoor amplification limit on its liquor licensees? I'd like this on the record before the next license renewal.

subject: Pothole cluster on Marshall Ave near 22nd is wrecking cars
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, there's a nasty cluster of potholes on Marshall Ave between 22nd and 23rd, right by the school crossing. I bent a rim Friday morning and my neighbor says she blew a tire there last week. With all the rain we've had they've gotten deep fast — one is easily eight inches. School's out so there are kids on bikes cutting through too. Can you get a crew out to patch the whole stretch? I can mark the worst three spots with chalk if that helps the crew find them. Thanks for whatever you can do.

subject: My water bill tripled and I don't understand why
body_clean: Hello, my June water bill for 1119 Eastern Ave came in at $214 when I normally pay around $70. Account number 19-2207. I haven't filled a pool, I don't have a sprinkler system, and there's no water running anywhere I can find. Could the meter be misread, or is there a leak I'm not seeing? I'm a single mom and a bill this size really hurts. Is there someone who can come check the meter, and is there a payment plan if it turns out the charge is correct? I'd appreciate a call back at the number on file. Thank you.

subject: Missed garbage pickup on Geneva Ave — second week running
body_clean: Mayor, the garbage truck skipped the 2100 block of Geneva Ave again on Thursday. This is the second Thursday in a row. Bins were at the curb by 6:30am both times. In this heat the cans are starting to smell and we've got raccoons getting into them at night. The recycling got taken fine, just not the regular trash. Can you send a makeup truck and figure out why our block keeps getting passed? A few neighbors are asking me about it because I posted on the village Facebook group. Appreciate any help.

subject: Fence permit status — submitted three weeks ago
body_clean: Hi, I submitted a fence permit application (permit #P-26-0488) for 1208 Rice Ave on June 2nd along with my plat of survey and the contractor's license info. It's been three weeks and I haven't heard anything. My contractor has us scheduled for the first week of July and he won't start without the approved permit in hand. Is there something missing from my application, or is it just in the queue? I want a 5-foot cedar privacy fence along the back property line, nothing unusual. Can someone let me know where it stands? I can come into the building department in person if that moves it along.

subject: Large tree limb hanging over sidewalk on Hyde Park Ave
body_clean: Mayor, Saturday's storm cracked a big limb on the parkway tree in front of 723 Hyde Park Ave. It's hung up but barely, dangling maybe ten feet over the public sidewalk, and it groans every time the wind picks up. Kids walk under it to get to the park. I'm not sure if that's a village tree or mine to deal with since it's on the parkway strip. Can someone come assess it before it comes down on somebody? I'd take it down myself but it's tangled in the utility line and I'm not going near that. Please advise who handles parkway trees.

subject: Cars parked on the lawn next door at 832 Bohland
body_clean: Hello Mayor, I don't like complaining about neighbors but I've tried talking to them and gotten nowhere. The house next to me at 832 Bohland Ave has three or four cars parked on the front lawn most days now — tire ruts, dead grass, oil stains running toward the sidewalk. It's been getting worse all spring and now there's a car up on jack stands that hasn't moved in a month. I thought parking on an unpaved surface wasn't allowed in the village. Could code enforcement take a look? I'd rather not have my name attached if there's any way to keep this anonymous, since we have to live next to each other.

subject: Broken swing and standing water at Memorial Park playground
body_clean: Hi there, I take my two little ones to the Memorial Park playground most mornings and wanted to flag a couple of safety issues. One of the baby swings has a cracked chain link and is hanging crooked — looks like it could give way. And after this weekend's rain there's a big puddle that never drains under the slide, so the whole landing area is mud by mid-morning. Also the water fountain by the pavilion isn't working. With summer camp groups starting to use the park it's getting busy. Could the parks crew take a look this week? Happy to send photos. Thanks for keeping our parks nice.

subject: Faded stop sign / sun glare at 19th and Washington
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the stop sign for northbound traffic at 19th Ave and Washington Blvd is so faded it's nearly pink, and in the late afternoon the sun is right behind it so drivers blow through. I've nearly been T-boned twice this month pulling out of my driveway. There's also a tree branch from the corner lot partly blocking it. With kids out for the summer and more people walking, this feels like an accident waiting to happen. Can the village replace the sign with a fresh reflective one and trim back whatever is blocking the sight line? I'd be glad to meet a crew out there to point out the angle.

subject: Final bill / closing account at 1455 Frederick Ave
body_clean: Hello, we're selling our home at 1455 Frederick Ave and closing is set for July 10th. I need to know the process for getting a final water meter read and closing out account 14-5512 so there's no carryover to the buyers, and so I get any deposit refund owed. The title company is asking for a final water bill or a paid-in-full letter from the village. Who do I contact, and how much lead time do you need to schedule the read? We've been good customers for nine years and want to leave everything clean for the new owners. Thank you for the help.

subject: How do I get a bulk pickup for old furniture?
body_clean: Hi, we're cleaning out my late mother's house at 2033 50th Ave and have a couch, a recliner, an old mattress, and a few boxes of junk to get rid of. What's the process for a bulk item pickup in Bellwood — is it included with our regular service or is there a fee per item? And do I need to call ahead or just set it out on garbage day with a sticker? I want to do this the right way and not get a ticket for dumping. We're hoping to have the house cleared out by the end of the month. Thanks for pointing me in the right direction.
